note: Catalog of civic discovery probes for the OpenSkagit assessor/GIS database. Each entry is a metadata template; the discovery agent generates the query, runs it, and attaches a 'rows' array at runtime. 'signal' describes the data pattern in plain terms (no hard-coded column names) so the agent can map it to the live schema. 'quality_rules' guard against false positives and overclaims; 'minimum_evidence' gates whether a probe may produce a publishable idea; 'publish_rules' must all pass before an idea enters 'ideas'; 'reject_rules' push candidates into 'rejected_ideas'. Treat all outputs as leads unless evidence is strong. Probes flagged in 'avoid' as needing data beyond core assessor tables (GIS overlays, ACS, permits, tax/payment, history snapshots) should be skipped or degraded gracefully if that source is unavailable.

probes.description: high tax productivity parcels, filtered to sane acreage bands
probes.civic_question: Which parcels produce the most assessed value per acre, and where are they?
probes.signal: total assessed value divided by acreage, ranked descending, clipped to reasonable acreage bands to drop slivers and giant rural tracts
avoid: comparing improved and unimproved parcels in the same band
treating high $/acre as 'good' without land-use context
quality_rules: Clip to defined acreage bands before ranking; slivers and common-area fragments produce false $/acre spikes.
Rank within a single land-use class; a retail block and a utility substation are not one cohort.
Exclude current-use, exempt, water, and ROW parcels from 'productivity' claims unless the idea is explicitly about them.
publish_rules: Must rank within a land-use cohort, not across all uses.
Must report the acreage band used so readers can judge the cut.
Frame as where land works hardest, not as a verdict on under- or over-development.
reject_rules: Top rows are slivers, common areas, or condo artifacts.
Finding rests on a single parcel.

probes.description: land value much higher than building value
probes.civic_question: Where is the land worth far more than what sits on it?
probes.signal: land value / improvement value ratio, surfaced where land dominates and improvement is minimal
avoid: assuming owner intent to develop
flagging parks, easements, or common-area parcels as 'underused'
quality_rules: A high land/improvement ratio is normal for parking, ag, and holding land; separate these before calling a parcel underused.
Exclude common-area, ROW, and water parcels where improvement value is structurally near zero.
Do not infer intent to develop or to speculate from the ratio alone.
publish_rules: Must group by land use or area rather than listing raw extremes.
Must note plausible benign explanations such as parking, ag, or conservation.
Frame as a redevelopment lead, not a judgment.
reject_rules: Evidence is dominated by parcels with structurally zero improvement (water, ROW, common area).
Idea asserts speculation or hoarding without ownership or holding-period evidence.

probes.description: zoning category differs from actual assessor land use
probes.civic_question: Where does the zoning on the books not match how land is actually used?
probes.signal: join zoning layer to assessor land-use code; surface rows where the two disagree by category
avoid: assuming mismatch is illegal
ignoring legal nonconforming and pending rezones
quality_rules: Treat mismatches as planning questions, not violations.
Separate legal nonconforming use, pending rezones, public uses, and mixed-use parcels where possible.
Do not compare broad zoning labels to assessor land-use labels without category grouping.
publish_rules: Must frame the mismatch as a land-use or planning pattern.
Must avoid implying illegality or owner fault.
Must identify what zoning or land-use category drives the pattern.
reject_rules: Zoning and land-use labels are too ambiguous to compare.

probes.description: recent sale price far above or below assessed value
probes.civic_question: Where are market prices diverging from assessed values?
probes.signal: recent qualified sale price / assessed value at time of sale; surface large deviations either direction
avoid: accusing assessor error from one sale
including non-arm's-length or unqualified sales
quality_rules: Exclude tiny parcels, water areas, rights-of-way, and common-area artifacts from publishable assessment-gap claims unless the idea is explicitly about data quality.
Do not infer tax fairness, under-taxation, or assessor error from individual sale ratios.
Separate vacant/unimproved land from improved residential or commercial property before comparing ratios.
publish_rules: Must describe a pattern across a cohort, not a single extreme sale.
Must state whether the pattern is by land use, neighborhood, sale period, or property type.
Must include a next check before publication.
reject_rules: Main evidence comes from tiny parcels or parcel artifacts.
Claim implies assessor error or tax unfairness without cohort support.

probes.description: valuable land with little/no improvement value
probes.civic_question: Where is high-value land sitting empty?
probes.signal: high land value combined with near-zero improvement value
avoid: counting protected open space or critical-area parcels as 'empty'
quality_rules: Confirm 'vacant' from near-zero improvement value, not just a land-use label.
Exclude conservation, current-use, ROW, water, and common-area parcels from 'empty valuable land' claims.
Do not imply land banking or speculation without ownership or holding-period evidence.
publish_rules: Must distinguish developable vacant land from protected or unbuildable land.
Must group by area or zoning to show a pattern.
Frame as infill opportunity, not neglect.
reject_rules: Parcels are protected, current-use, or physically unbuildable.
Claim asserts speculation without holding evidence.

probes.description: low tax yield parcels in service-heavy areas
probes.civic_question: Which low-yield parcels sit in the most service-intensive areas?
probes.signal: low total tax/value parcels located inside dense or high-service districts (city core, urban fire/sewer)
avoid: ignoring exempt parcels that deliver public benefit
treating low yield as waste
quality_rules: Exempt and public parcels often show low yield by design; flag them separately rather than calling them waste.
Service intensity here is a district/density proxy, not a measured cost; label it as such.
Do not imply a parcel should be taxed more or be developed.
publish_rules: Must state the service-intensity proxy used.
Must separate exempt/public parcels from private low-yield parcels.
Frame as a fiscal-pattern lead, not a recommendation to tax or develop.
reject_rules: Evidence is mostly exempt or public land treated as waste.
Service cost asserted as a dollar figure without cost data.

probes.description: similar parcels just inside/outside city limits
probes.civic_question: How do comparable parcels differ on either side of a city line?
probes.signal: pair parcels of similar land use/size that straddle a municipal boundary; compare value, tax, zoning
avoid: over-matching dissimilar parcels
ignoring different service packages
quality_rules: Match parcels on land use, size, and rough vintage before comparing across the line.
Different service packages explain part of any tax gap; note this rather than implying unfairness.
Avoid pairing parcels from very different neighborhoods just because they straddle a boundary.
publish_rules: Must describe the matching criteria used for the comparison.
Must attribute differences to plausible causes (services, zoning), not motive.
Frame as a tradeoff comparison, not a fairness verdict.
reject_rules: Compared parcels are not actually comparable.
Claim implies one jurisdiction is cheating residents.

probes.description: assessed value in flood/critical-area overlays
probes.civic_question: How much assessed value sits inside flood and critical-area overlays?
probes.signal: sum assessed value of parcels intersecting FEMA flood / critical-area GIS layers, by district
avoid: implying every flood-zone parcel is at imminent risk
ignoring elevation/dike protection
quality_rules: Overlay intersection is approximate at parcel edges; treat boundary parcels as uncertain.
Distinguish flood-zone presence from actual risk (dikes, elevation); do not equate them.
Aggregate value; do not single out individual owners' exposure.
publish_rules: Must report exposure as a district or area aggregate.
Must name the overlay layer and its date.
Frame as exposure, not predicted loss.
reject_rules: Claim predicts inundation or loss for specific parcels.
Boundary-overlap parcels drive the result without a buffer check.
Overlay layer is unknown or undated.

probes.description: parcel/tax patterns by district
probes.civic_question: How do tax patterns and value differ across school and fire districts?
probes.signal: aggregate value and effective tax by school/fire district; compare per-parcel and per-value rates
avoid: comparing rates without service-level context
quality_rules: Effective-rate differences reflect different service levels and voter-approved levies; note this.
Compare within comparable district types; do not put school rates against fire rates directly.
Do not imply a district is mismanaged from rate alone.
publish_rules: Must compare like district types.
Must pair each rate with its service or levy context.
Frame as a funding pattern, not a verdict.
reject_rules: Fewer than a few districts compared.

probes.description: market pressure by neighborhood
probes.civic_question: Which neighborhoods are seeing the most sales activity and price movement?
probes.signal: count and median price of qualified sales in a recent window, grouped by neighborhood
avoid: reading thin-sample neighborhoods as trends
quality_rules: Use qualified arm's-length sales only; drop family transfers and non-market sales.
Suppress neighborhoods below a minimum sale count to avoid thin-sample swings.
Use median over mean to limit distortion from a single high sale.
publish_rules: Must state the time window and the sale count per neighborhood.
Must suppress or label thin-sample neighborhoods.
Frame as market activity, not a guaranteed price direction.
reject_rules: Neighborhood has too few sales to be stable.

probes.description: tax-exempt or publicly held land by city/district
probes.civic_question: How much land and value is publicly held or tax-exempt, by area?
probes.signal: share of parcel count, acreage, and value flagged exempt or public-owned, grouped by city/district
avoid: framing exempt land as lost revenue without weighing public benefit
quality_rules: Exempt status is intentional; present as composition, not lost revenue.
Separate publicly owned from privately owned exempt (nonprofit) parcels where possible.
Do not imply exempt land should be taxed.
publish_rules: Must present as share of base by area.
Must distinguish public vs nonprofit exempt where the data allows.
Frame neutrally as tax-base composition.
reject_rules: Framed as revenue lost to exemptions.
Public and nonprofit exempt conflated when they could be separated.

probes.description: parcels where current use is below zoning capacity
probes.civic_question: Where is current use well below what zoning would allow?
probes.signal: compare allowed density/intensity from zoning to current improvement intensity per parcel
avoid: ignoring infrastructure, access, and critical-area constraints
quality_rules: Zoned capacity is a theoretical max, not buildable yield; label it as such.
Exclude parcels constrained by critical areas, access, or fragmentation before claiming capacity.
Do not imply owners are obligated to build.
publish_rules: Must state that capacity is zoned theoretical, not net buildable.
Must note major known constraints.
Frame as a capacity lead for planning.
reject_rules: Capacity reported as buildable without a constraint check.

probes.description: sales-ratio spread across neighborhoods
probes.civic_question: Does the assessed-to-sale ratio vary systematically between neighborhoods?
probes.signal: median assessed/sale ratio per neighborhood from qualified sales; compare dispersion
avoid: drawing conclusions from neighborhoods with few sales
quality_rules: Use qualified sales only and a recent window for the ratio.
Suppress neighborhoods below a minimum sale count; small samples create fake spread.
Report as an indicator, not a formal ratio study or a finding of inequity.
publish_rules: Must report sale counts per neighborhood.
Must frame as a uniformity indicator that a formal study would need to confirm.
Must avoid claiming intentional inequity.
reject_rules: Neighborhood sale counts too low to be stable.
Presented as a finished ratio study.

probes.description: lower-value homes assessed at a higher share of market
probes.civic_question: Are lower-priced homes assessed closer to (or above) market than higher-priced ones?
probes.signal: regress assessed/sale ratio against sale price; surface the slope and tails
avoid: claiming regressivity without a proper ratio study
conflating with neighborhood quality
quality_rules: Regressivity requires a price-versus-ratio relationship across many sales, never a handful.
Use qualified sales and a recent window; control for property type where possible.
Frame strictly as a lead for a formal ratio study; do not state regressivity as fact.
publish_rules: Must report sample size and the direction of the price-ratio relationship.
Must label as preliminary, pending a formal IAAO-style study.
Must avoid asserting assessor intent.
reject_rules: Sample too small for a credible slope.

probes.description: senior/disabled/nonprofit exemptions clustered by district
probes.civic_question: Where are property-tax exemptions concentrated?
probes.signal: count and value of exemption types grouped by district/neighborhood
avoid: implying exemptions are abuse
naming individuals
quality_rules: Exemptions are lawful programs; present concentration as a demographic or program pattern, not abuse.
Aggregate by district; never name or profile individual exemption holders.
Separate exemption types (senior/disabled vs nonprofit), since they mean different things.
publish_rules: Must aggregate, with no individuals named.
Must separate exemption types.
Frame as a program or equity pattern.

probes.description: share of land area off the rolls per area
probes.civic_question: What fraction of land is off the tax rolls in each city or district?
probes.signal: exempt acreage / total acreage by jurisdiction
avoid: ignoring the public function of exempt land
quality_rules: Off-the-rolls acreage is mostly intentional public or conservation land; present it neutrally.
Water, ROW, and tideland acreage can distort the share; note their inclusion.
Do not frame the share as a problem to fix.
publish_rules: Must state what land categories the exempt acreage includes.
Must present as composition by jurisdiction.
Frame neutrally.
reject_rules: Framed as land that should be taxed.

probes.description: largest year-over-year assessed value change
probes.civic_question: Where is assessed value rising fastest?
probes.signal: year-over-year change in assessed value by parcel/neighborhood, ranked
avoid: reading a revaluation-cycle catch-up as real-time appreciation
quality_rules: Revaluation cycles cause step changes unrelated to current market; separate reval years.
Use percent and absolute change together; small bases inflate percentages.
Frame as where assessments rose, not as proof of displacement.
publish_rules: Must note whether a revaluation occurred in the window.
Must show both percent and absolute change.
Frame as a growth/pressure lead, with displacement raised only as a question.
reject_rules: Change is a reval artifact mistaken for appreciation.
Percentage spikes driven by tiny bases.

probes.description: commercial-zoned parcels with low improvement value
probes.civic_question: Which commercial corridors are underbuilt relative to their zoning?
probes.signal: commercial-zoned parcels with low improvement value or single-story footprints along key corridors
avoid: assuming owner intent
flagging working low-rise businesses as failures
quality_rules: Low improvement value can be a healthy small business; do not call it failure.
Confirm commercial zoning and corridor location, not just a label.
Do not infer owner intent or imply blight.
publish_rules: Must tie parcels to a named corridor or area.
Must frame as infill potential, not decline.
Must avoid blight language without condition data.
reject_rules: Working low-rise businesses framed as failures.
Parcels not actually on a commercial corridor.

probes.description: large low-improvement commercial parcels near the core
probes.civic_question: How much prime near-core land is surface parking or pavement?
probes.signal: commercial parcels with high land value, very low improvement value, and large footprint near downtown
avoid: misclassifying legitimately needed parking or staging land
quality_rules: Confirm low improvement plus large footprint near the core; do not assume use from a label.
Some parking is required or genuinely needed; do not imply it must be redeveloped.
Exclude structured-parking and mixed-use parcels.
publish_rules: Must locate parcels relative to the core or downtown.
Frame as a land-efficiency lead, not a demand to build.
Must acknowledge legitimate parking needs.
reject_rules: Parcels are structured parking or mixed-use.
Claim asserts parcels must be redeveloped.

probes.description: improvement year-built distribution by area
probes.civic_question: Where is the housing stock oldest and likely due for reinvestment?
probes.signal: distribution of improvement year-built by neighborhood/district
avoid: equating age with disrepair
ignoring remodels not reflected in year-built
quality_rules: Year-built misses remodels and additions; age is not condition.
Aggregate distributions by area; do not flag individual homes as old or distressed.
Do not imply disrepair, code issues, or owner neglect.
publish_rules: Must present age as an area distribution.
Must state that age does not equal condition.
Frame as a reinvestment or program-targeting lead.
reject_rules: Individual homes labeled distressed from age.

probes.description: manufactured-housing communities by value and district
probes.civic_question: Where are manufactured-housing communities, and what is their exposure?
probes.signal: parcels classed as manufactured-home parks; value, district, and sale activity
avoid: stigmatizing residents
assuming sale means closure
quality_rules: Treat residents as a vulnerable population; never stigmatize or name them.
A park sale or value change is not a closure; do not imply displacement without more.
Confirm park classification rather than inferring it from value alone.
publish_rules: Must aggregate at the park or community level.
Must frame as a preservation or affordability lead.
Must avoid predicting closure or displacement from value alone.
reject_rules: Predicts closure or displacement from a single signal.

probes.description: multifamily parcels counted and valued by area
probes.civic_question: How is multifamily housing distributed across the county?
probes.signal: count and value of multifamily-classed parcels grouped by city/district
avoid: treating parcel count as unit count without a unit field
quality_rules: Parcel count is not unit count without a unit field; label which you actually have.
Confirm multifamily classification; mixed-use can be miscounted.
Aggregate by district; avoid single-building claims.
publish_rules: Must state whether counts are parcels or units.
Must aggregate by district.
Frame as a supply-distribution lead.
reject_rules: Parcel count presented as unit count.

probes.description: single-family lots large enough for an ADU under zoning
probes.civic_question: How many lots could plausibly add an accessory dwelling unit?
probes.signal: single-family parcels meeting minimum lot-size/coverage thresholds for ADUs under current code
avoid: ignoring utility, access, and critical-area limits that block real feasibility
quality_rules: Lot-size eligibility is necessary, not sufficient; utilities, access, and critical areas gate real feasibility.
Use current code thresholds; do not assume future upzones.
Frame as theoretical capacity, never as a development plan for any parcel.
publish_rules: Must label results as theoretical eligibility, not feasibility.
Must note the constraints that were not checked.
Frame as a capacity estimate for planning.

probes.description: lots exceeding minimum size that could split
probes.civic_question: Which parcels are large enough to subdivide under current zoning?
probes.signal: parcels whose acreage exceeds the minimum lot size by a margin allowing a split
avoid: ignoring easements, critical areas, and access requirements
quality_rules: Acreage over the minimum is necessary, not sufficient; easements, access, and critical areas constrain splits.
Use current minimum lot sizes per zone; do not assume rezones.
Frame as theoretical split capacity, not a plat plan.
publish_rules: Must label as theoretical capacity.
Must note unchecked constraints.
Frame as a planning-capacity lead.

probes.description: ag/timber/open-space current-use parcels and deferral
probes.civic_question: How much land is enrolled in current-use (open space/ag/timber) programs?
probes.signal: parcels flagged in current-use programs; deferred vs market value, by district
avoid: framing deferral as a loophole
ignoring the public conservation purpose
quality_rules: Deferral is a lawful conservation program; never frame it as a loophole or evasion.
Compare deferred vs market value as program scale, not as lost revenue.
Aggregate by district; avoid singling out enrolled owners.
publish_rules: Must present enrollment as program scale by area.
Must frame deferral as intended conservation policy.
Must avoid lost-revenue framing.
reject_rules: Framed as a loophole or evasion.

probes.description: farmland near city/UGA edge with high value-per-acre
probes.civic_question: Where is farmland under the most conversion pressure?
probes.signal: ag-classed parcels adjacent to city limits or UGA with elevated value/acre or recent sales
avoid: assuming a sale equals conversion
ignoring current-use protections
quality_rules: Proximity and value/acre indicate pressure, not actual conversion; a sale is not a conversion.
Current-use enrollment lowers assessed value; account for it before reading 'high value'.
Frame as a watch list, not a prediction of farmland loss.
publish_rules: Must frame as conversion pressure, not conversion.
Must account for current-use effects on value.
Frame as a monitoring lead.

probes.description: Urban Growth Area parcels underutilized vs zoning
probes.civic_question: Is the UGA actually absorbing growth, or sitting underused?
probes.signal: parcels inside UGA boundaries with current use below zoned capacity
avoid: ignoring infrastructure phasing and ownership fragmentation
quality_rules: UGA boundary intersection is approximate; treat edge parcels as uncertain.
Capacity is zoned theoretical; infrastructure phasing and ownership fragmentation limit real absorption.
Frame as a buildable-lands question, not a verdict on growth management.
publish_rules: Must label capacity as theoretical.
Must note infrastructure and fragmentation caveats.
Frame as a GMA monitoring lead.
reject_rules: Theoretical capacity treated as ready supply.
Edge parcels drive the result without a check.

probes.description: residential improvement value inside FEMA flood zones
probes.civic_question: How many homes and how much improvement value sit in flood zones?
probes.signal: residential improvement value for parcels intersecting FEMA flood layers
avoid: implying all flood-zone homes face imminent loss
ignoring dike protection and base flood elevation
quality_rules: Overlay intersection at parcel edges is uncertain; flag boundary parcels.
Flood-zone presence is not imminent risk; dikes and elevation matter.
Aggregate; do not publish individual homeowners' exposure.
publish_rules: Must aggregate exposure by area.
Must name the flood layer and its date.
Frame as exposure, not predicted damage.

probes.description: improvements overlapping wetlands/steep slopes/habitat
probes.civic_question: Where do existing improvements overlap mapped critical areas?
probes.signal: improved parcels whose footprint intersects critical-area GIS layers
avoid: assuming a violation from overlap alone
ignoring legal nonconforming and buffer rules
quality_rules: Overlap with a critical-area layer is not a violation; buffers and legal nonconforming status exist.
Overlay edges are imprecise; treat slivers of overlap as uncertain.
Never imply wrongdoing or illegal development.
publish_rules: Must frame overlaps as review questions, not violations.
Must note overlay precision limits.
Frame as an environmental-review lead.

probes.description: low-elevation Skagit delta/estuary parcels
probes.civic_question: What value sits on low-lying delta and estuary land?
probes.signal: parcels at low elevation near tidal/estuary GIS extents; assessed value summed by area
avoid: making precise inundation claims without elevation/LiDAR data
quality_rules: Without elevation/LiDAR, do not make inundation claims; proximity is a proxy.
Dike and tide-gate protection changes real exposure; note it.
Aggregate value; avoid singling out parcels.
publish_rules: Must label as proximity-based exposure, not inundation.
Must mention protection infrastructure where relevant.
Frame as an adaptation-planning lead.
reject_rules: Specific inundation predicted without elevation data.

probes.description: assessed value within diking/drainage districts
probes.civic_question: What tax base sits behind dikes and inside drainage districts?
probes.signal: sum assessed value of parcels inside diking/drainage district boundaries
avoid: assuming uniform risk across a district
quality_rules: A district boundary defines who pays, not uniform risk; do not equate the two.
Present as the base supporting the district, not as risk per parcel.
Aggregate within district boundaries.
publish_rules: Must present as a district tax-base aggregate.
Must avoid implying uniform per-parcel risk.
Frame as an infrastructure-funding lead.
reject_rules: Risk asserted uniformly across the district.
Individual parcels singled out for risk.

probes.description: parcels in the wildland-urban interface
probes.civic_question: How much value sits in the wildfire interface zone?
probes.signal: parcels intersecting WUI / fire-hazard GIS layers; value and count by fire district
avoid: overstating risk without fuel, slope, and access data
quality_rules: WUI layer membership is not a risk score; fuels, slope, and access determine real risk.
Overlay edges are imprecise; treat boundary parcels as uncertain.
Aggregate; do not assign risk to named owners.
publish_rules: Must aggregate value within the WUI layer by district.
Must state that membership is not measured risk.
Frame as a mitigation or funding lead.
reject_rules: Risk overstated from layer membership alone.

probes.description: total levy rate by levy code area
probes.civic_question: How does the combined levy rate vary across the county, and why?
probes.signal: total levy rate per levy code area, with the district stack that composes it
avoid: comparing headline rates without the underlying service mix
quality_rules: The headline rate hides the district stack and voter-approved levies; always show the components.
Compare areas with similar service mixes, not raw rates across unlike places.
Do not imply any area is overcharged from the rate alone.
publish_rules: Must break the rate into its district components.
Must compare like service mixes.
Frame as a transparency lead, not a verdict.
reject_rules: Rates compared without a component breakdown.

probes.description: assessed value per resident by district
probes.civic_question: How much taxable value backs each resident, by district?
probes.signal: district assessed value divided by ACS population for the same geography
avoid: using ACS at small geographies with large margins of error
stale population estimates
quality_rules: ACS population at small geographies has large margins of error; report the MOE or suppress.
District and ACS geographies rarely align perfectly; note the approximation.
Frame as a fiscal-capacity indicator, not precise per-person wealth.
publish_rules: Must report the ACS margin of error or suppress unstable estimates.
Must note geography-matching limits.
Frame as a capacity indicator.
reject_rules: Presented as an exact per-person figure.

probes.description: top parcels' share of a district's tax base
probes.civic_question: How dependent is each district on a handful of large taxpayers?
probes.signal: share of total district value held by the top N parcels/owners
avoid: naming or profiling individual owners carelessly
privacy overreach
quality_rules: Owner-name normalization is noisy; verify the grouping before claiming concentration.
Aggregate concentration is publishable; naming or profiling owners is not.
Concentration is a resilience question, not evidence of favoritism.
publish_rules: Must report the top-N share, not individual identities.
Must note owner-normalization uncertainty.
Frame as a fiscal-resilience lead.
reject_rules: Concentration claimed without an owner-dedup check.

probes.description: improvement value from recent new construction
probes.civic_question: Where is new construction adding the most assessed value?
probes.signal: improvement value tied to a new-construction flag or recent build year, grouped by area
avoid: confusing revaluation increases with genuine new construction
quality_rules: Separate genuine new-construction value from revaluation increases.
Use the new-construction flag or build year, not raw value change.
Aggregate by area; avoid single-project claims unless verified.
publish_rules: Must isolate new-construction value, not reval.
Must state the time window.
Frame as a growth or revenue lead.
reject_rules: Reval increases counted as new construction.

probes.description: permits issued vs assessed improvement change
probes.civic_question: How long after a permit does assessed value catch up?
probes.signal: match issued permits (iWorQ) to subsequent improvement-value changes; measure the lag
avoid: expecting alignment within a single cycle
ignoring permits that never build
quality_rules: Permit and assessment cycles differ; do not treat normal lag as a problem.
Match permits to parcels carefully; iWorQ-to-parcel joins can misalign.
Some permits never build; absence of value change is not always a lag.
publish_rules: Must describe the permit-to-parcel match method.
Must frame lag as expected cycle behavior unless extreme.
Frame as a process-visibility lead.
reject_rules: Normal cycle lag framed as failure.

probes.description: permits issued but no improvement value added
probes.civic_question: Which permitted projects show no sign of being built?
probes.signal: parcels with issued building permits but flat improvement value after a reasonable window
avoid: assuming a stall before normal assessment lag has passed
quality_rules: Wait past a normal assessment cycle before calling a project stalled.
Confirm the permit ties to the parcel and is a building permit, not a minor or utility permit.
Do not imply owner fault or financial trouble.
publish_rules: Must show the wait exceeds normal lag.
Must restrict to building permits matched to parcels.
Frame as a pipeline lead, not blame.
reject_rules: Permit type or match is wrong.

probes.description: many parcels under one owner entity
probes.civic_question: Who controls the most parcels, and where?
probes.signal: group parcels by normalized owner name/entity; count holdings and value by area
avoid: noisy name matching
treating distinct LLCs as separate without dedup
quality_rules: Normalize owner names before grouping; raw strings both over- and under-merge entities.
Distinct LLCs may share a beneficial owner you cannot see; do not overstate certainty.
Concentration is a market-structure question, not proof of wrongdoing.
publish_rules: Must describe the name-normalization method and its limits.
Must frame as a concentration pattern, not collusion.
Frame as a market-structure lead.
reject_rules: Claims hidden common ownership as fact.

probes.description: owner mailing address outside county/state
probes.civic_question: How much property is owned from outside the area?
probes.signal: compare owner mailing address geography to parcel location
avoid: PO boxes and property managers inflating the count
equating out-of-area with neglect
quality_rules: Mailing address is not residence; PO boxes and property managers inflate absentee counts.
Out-of-area ownership is not neglect or wrongdoing.
Aggregate by area; avoid targeting individuals.
publish_rules: Must note the mailing-address limitation.
Must aggregate by area.
Frame as an ownership-pattern lead.
reject_rules: Mailing address treated as proof of an absentee owner.

probes.description: churches, hospitals, schools — land and value share
probes.civic_question: What footprint do anchor institutions hold in each community?
probes.signal: parcels classed institutional/nonprofit; land, value, and acreage share by area
avoid: implying exemption is improper
lumping unlike institutions together
quality_rules: Institutional exemption is lawful; present footprint as composition, not lost revenue.
Do not lump unlike institutions (hospital vs church vs school) without grouping.
Aggregate; avoid singling out one institution unless it is the clear, verified story.
publish_rules: Must group by institution type.
Must present as base composition.
Frame neutrally.
reject_rules: Framed as revenue lost to exemptions.

probes.description: parcels within trust or reservation boundaries
probes.civic_question: How does land within reservation/trust boundaries sit relative to county rolls?
probes.signal: parcels intersecting Swinomish / Upper Skagit / Sauk-Suiattle boundary layers; tax status context
avoid: misstating sovereignty or tax status
treating this as a fairness gap
quality_rules: Tribal trust and reservation land has distinct sovereign tax status; state it accurately or omit.
Do not frame trust-land tax status as a gap, loophole, or unfairness.
Defer to authoritative boundary and status sources; flag uncertainty.
publish_rules: Must state tax and jurisdiction status accurately and neutrally.
Must rely on authoritative boundary data.
Frame as context, not a fairness comparison.
reject_rules: Frames trust land as a fairness gap.
Boundary or status data is uncertain.

probes.description: county parcels adjacent to or inside a UGA, unannexed
probes.civic_question: Which county parcels are logical annexation candidates for a city?
probes.signal: parcels inside or abutting a UGA but outside city limits
avoid: assuming residents want annexation
ignoring fiscal impact on the city
quality_rules: Adjacency to a UGA is a planning signal, not resident or city intent.
Annexation changes services and taxes both ways; note this rather than implying it is obviously good.
Confirm parcels are unannexed and within or abutting the UGA.
publish_rules: Must frame as candidates by geography, not an endorsement.
Must note service and tax tradeoffs.
Frame as a planning lead.
reject_rules: Parcels not actually adjacent or unannexed.

probes.description: low-density parcels far from the service core
probes.civic_question: Where does scattered low-density development likely cost more to serve than it returns?
probes.signal: low value-per-acre parcels at distance from the urban core / utility extents (density + distance proxy)
avoid: claiming exact service cost without infrastructure cost data — this is a proxy only
quality_rules: This is a density-and-distance proxy, never a measured service cost; label it explicitly.
Rural low density can be appropriate; do not imply it is a fiscal failure.
Exclude current-use, conservation, and exempt parcels from cost framing.
publish_rules: Must state that cost is a proxy, with no dollar figure implied.
Must exclude protected and exempt parcels from the cost framing.
Frame as a fiscal-pattern lead for further study.
reject_rules: Proxy presented as a dollar service cost.
Rural land framed as failure without cost data.

probes.description: water-adjacent parcels and their value premium
probes.civic_question: How large is the waterfront value premium, and who holds it?
probes.signal: compare value/acre of water-adjacent parcels to comparable inland parcels
avoid: conflating view parcels with actual frontage
ignoring shoreline regulation effects
quality_rules: Separate true frontage from view-only parcels; they carry different premiums.
Shoreline regulation and access limits affect value; note them.
Match to comparable inland parcels before stating a premium.
publish_rules: Must define frontage vs view in the comparison.
Must describe the inland comparison group.
Frame as a land-economics or access-equity lead.

probes.description: tiny parcels with outsized value
probes.civic_question: Which small lots punch far above their size in value?
probes.signal: low acreage parcels in the top value/acre tier (downtown, waterfront)
avoid: condo and common-area parcel artifacts skewing results
quality_rules: Condo, common-area, and unit parcels create false small-lot/high-value artifacts; exclude them.
Confirm the parcel is a real standalone lot, not a fractional interest.
Frame as a land-efficiency observation, not a target.
publish_rules: Must exclude condo and common-area artifacts.
Must group by area or use.
Frame as an efficiency lead.
reject_rules: Results dominated by condo or common-area parcels.

probes.description: big acreage with low total value
probes.civic_question: Where is large acreage carrying very low value?
probes.signal: high-acreage parcels in the bottom value tier (timber, undeveloped, current use)
avoid: ignoring current-use deferral that intentionally lowers assessed value
quality_rules: Current-use and timber deferral intentionally lower assessed value; account for it before reading 'low value'.
Distinguish unbuildable or protected acreage from developable holdings.
Do not imply land banking without holding evidence.
publish_rules: Must account for current-use deferral effects.
Must separate protected from developable acreage.
Frame as an open-space or holding lead.
reject_rules: Deferral-lowered value read as a finding.

probes.description: dispersion of assessed value within neighborhoods
probes.civic_question: How unequal is property value within and across neighborhoods?
probes.signal: value dispersion (e.g., Gini or quintile spread) by neighborhood
avoid: treating assessed value as a direct measure of resident wealth
quality_rules: Assessed value is not resident wealth or income; do not equate them.
Suppress neighborhoods with too few parcels for a stable dispersion measure.
Frame dispersion as a mapping observation, not a social judgment.
publish_rules: Must state that assessed value is not wealth.
Must suppress thin neighborhoods.
Frame as an equity-mapping lead.
reject_rules: Value dispersion presented as wealth inequality.

probes.description: ACS rent burden vs multifamily value by tract
probes.civic_question: Do high rent-burden areas align with the multifamily stock that serves them?
probes.signal: overlay ACS rent-burden by tract with multifamily parcel value/location
avoid: ACS small-area margins of error
tract boundaries not matching neighborhoods
quality_rules: ACS tract estimates carry large margins of error; report MOE or suppress unstable tracts.
Tract boundaries seldom match neighborhoods or parcels; note the approximation.
Frame alignment as a question, not a causal link between supply and burden.
publish_rules: Must report ACS margins or suppress shaky tracts.
Must note geography-matching limits.
Frame as an affordability lead, not causation.
reject_rules: Unstable ACS tracts used without MOE.

probes.description: parcels missing land use, zoning, or value fields
probes.civic_question: Where is the assessment data incomplete in ways that affect fairness or analysis?
probes.signal: parcels with null or default values in land use, zoning, area, or value fields
avoid: treating valid nulls (common areas, ROW) as errors
quality_rules: Many nulls are valid (common areas, ROW, water, new parcels); separate expected nulls from true gaps.
Frame as internal QA, not as evidence of unfair assessment, unless gaps cluster meaningfully.
Aggregate gap rates by field and area; avoid singling out parcels.
publish_rules: Must distinguish valid nulls from real gaps.
Must report gap rates by field and area.
Frame as a data-integrity lead.
reject_rules: Implies unfair assessment without clustering evidence.
